featured.title: Currency Exchange in Berlin: Where to Change Money (and Where Not To) in 2026
featured.label: Newest money guide
featured.summary: Most visitors do not need to exchange cash in Berlin at all. A local guide to getting euros without the hidden margin: bank ATMs, the airport-desk trap, the dynamic-conversion question, and how much cash to actually carry.
featured.alt: Close-up of euro banknotes, a fifty and a twenty, the cash most visitors think they need to exchange before arriving in Berlin

miniPosts.title: Mauerpark on a Sunday: Berlin's Best Free Flea Market and Karaoke (2026)
miniPosts.summary: A Berlin guide's take on Mauerpark on a Sunday: the free flea market, the open-air Bearpit Karaoke, the best times to arrive, and how to shape a Berlin Sunday around it.
miniPosts.alt: A large crowd fills the stone amphitheatre at Mauerpark for the Sunday Bearpit Karaoke, with the green park behind them

miniPosts.title: U-Bahn vs S-Bahn in Berlin: What's the Difference?
miniPosts.summary: A blue U, a green S and a row of numbers on the same wall. A local guide to the U-Bahn vs S-Bahn difference: which is which, which to take, and why one ticket covers both.
miniPosts.alt: Berlin S-Bahn train on the red-brick Stadtbahn viaduct curving past the domes and rooftops of the city centre near Hackescher Markt

miniPosts.title: Gendarmenmarkt: A Local Guide to Berlin's Most Beautiful Square (2026)
miniPosts.summary: Two of Gendarmenmarkt's three landmarks are called cathedrals, and neither one is. A local guide to Berlin's most beautiful square: what each building really is, the free exhibition inside the German Cathedral, and whether the French tower climb is worth it.
miniPosts.alt: Wide view across Gendarmenmarkt in Berlin, with the Konzerthaus on the left, the Schiller monument in the middle, and the French Cathedral with its green domed tower on the right

miniPosts.title: Air Conditioning in Berlin: Do Hotels Have It, and How to Stay Cool (2026)
miniPosts.summary: A Berlin guide's honest take on air conditioning in Berlin: why so few places have it, how to check before you book, and how to keep a no-AC room liveable in a summer heatwave.
miniPosts.alt: A classic Berlin Altbau room in summer with two tall open casement windows, sheer curtains, potted plants on the sill, an old cast-iron radiator, herringbone parquet floor and a small white electric fan by the window, warm afternoon sunlight across the floor

posts.title: Jewish Museum Berlin: Free Entry, How Long and What to See (2026)
posts.summary: A practical first-visit guide to the Jewish Museum Berlin: free entry, how long to allow, what to see first, security, accessibility and a route through Libeskind's building.
posts.alt: Baroque Old Building and zinc-clad Libeskind Building of the Jewish Museum Berlin seen from Lindenstrasse.

posts.title: Markthalle Neun: Berlin's Best Food Market and Street Food Thursday (2026)
posts.summary: Same market hall, completely different day. A local guide to Markthalle Neun in Kreuzberg: when Street Food Thursday runs, which day has the Big Market, and why you should never turn up on a Sunday.
posts.alt: The green cast-iron and glass interior of Markthalle Neun in Berlin-Kreuzberg, with hanging black-and-white portraits and food stalls under an arched roof

posts.title: Where to Watch the 2026 World Cup in Berlin (No, There's No Brandenburg Gate Fan Mile This Year)
posts.summary: The 2026 World Cup is in North America, and Berlin has no Brandenburg Gate fan mile this year. Here is where to actually watch: free screens, beer gardens and bars, with every kick-off in Berlin time.
posts.alt: A summer evening football public viewing in Berlin with a relaxed crowd, outdoor screen and TV Tower skyline
